./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:52:{"id":"corpus-368f5152b39b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the work / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-d3a4f89c","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:211:{"id":"corpus-e3d55d44b044","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read work-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:231:{"id":"corpus-f744aa57e91b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple work workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-55f05e07","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple work workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e"}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:238:{"id":"corpus-011259e83fe4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"work\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","bug","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-05b4cc9d","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"work\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","bug","graphql"]}}

DDx may keep copied/linkable evidence-envelope references and forward Fizeau transcript `ServiceEvent`s as opaque evidence, but it is only a pass-through/marshalling consumer for those events. Fizeau owns transcript, session-log, and progress rendering plus transcript semantics. DDx only preserves the opaque envelope and attachments; it does not rehydrate, parse, render, or semantically interpret inner Fizeau transcript or session-log content, and it does not render inner session logs in DDx worker views.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde::Serialize;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\\n    jwt_verify_layer,\\n};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ISSUER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"test-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"user-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"super-secret-key-for-tests\\\";\\nconst TEST_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_claims() -\u003e JwtClaims {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e MemoryStorageAdapter {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Test User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer_req(path: \u0026str, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n    json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde::Serialize;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\\n    jwt_verify_layer,\\n};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ISSUER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"test-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"user-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"super-secret-key-for-tests\\\";\\nconst TEST_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_claims() -\u003e JwtClaims {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e MemoryStorageAdapter {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Test User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer_req(path: \u0026str, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n    json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\\n}\\n\\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"unauthenticated\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\\n assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"credential_malformed\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\\n     databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    };\\n    let token =   .unwrap();\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \u0026token);\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. # Changelog

All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.

## [Unreleased]

### Added: Prose quality support user guide

DDx now includes user-facing prose-quality documentation at
[`docs/prose-quality.md`](docs/prose-quality.md). The guide explains the
changed-prose command surface, configuration knobs, example findings, and the
human-writing-support workflow. It also states explicitly that DDx prose
quality support is not an AI detector and not detector bypass.

### Added: Fizeau v0.10.13 point release

DDx now consumes `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.13`.

This release carries the sticky route and endpoint utilization evidence
surfaces that DDx agent routing and observability already consume, including
route status and session evidence plumbing. No DDx-side contract changes were
needed beyond the dependency pin refresh and test fixture updates for Fizeau's
canonical embedded `fiz` harness name.

### Added: MinPower/MaxPower passthrough contract (agent v0.9.25)

DDx now consumes the upstream `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` power-bounds
interface introduced in FEAT-006 / CONTRACT-003.

**What changed:**

- `cli/go.mod` pins `agent v0.9.25`, which exposes `MinPower`/`MaxPower` on
  `ServiceExecuteRequest`, `Power int` on `ModelInfo`, and per-candidate
  `Components.Power` in `routing_decision` events.
- `--min-power` / `--max-power` flags added to `ddx agent execute-bead` and
  `ddx agent work` (alias `ddx work`). Values are passed to
  `ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower/MaxPower` unchanged; DDx does not interpret
  them — the agent owns model selection within the bounds.
- `result.ActualPower` is now populated from the `routing_decision` event's
  winning candidate `Components.Power` and appears in `result.json` artifacts.
- `ddx agent models` now prints each model's power number when non-zero.
- `GET /api/agent/models` already returns `ModelInfo` which includes `Power`;
  no server-side change was required.

**No profile aliases or DDx-side route decisions are added.** The power bounds
are an opaque passthrough envelope between DDx and the agent's routing layer.

### Breaking changes

#### Removed: `agent.routing.default_harness`

The `agent.routing.default_harness` field has been removed from `.ddx/config.yaml`
(bead ddx-87fb72c2). DDx now refuses to load any config that still carries it
and prints a migration pointer instead.

Why: `default_harness` was a silent fallback that competed with the top-level
`agent.harness` setting and the live route-resolution call graph. It produced
confusing routing decisions when the upstream service had no viable provider for
the requested profile. The endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) makes
harness selection an explicit decision per dispatch — a config-level "default"
no longer fits.

Migration: delete the field. The top-level `agent.harness` is still honored as
a tie-break preference on the resolution path (NOT a default override). See
[docs/migrations/routing-config.md](docs/migrations/routing-config.md).

#### Removed: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`

These fields and the `--escalate` / `--override-model` CLI flags are removed
(bead ddx-3bd7396a). DDx refuses to load any config that still carries them.

Migration: delete the fields. The default work path issues exactly one
`ResolveRoute` call per bead; tier-ladder iteration is gone. See
[docs/migrations/routing-config.md](docs/migrations/routing-config.md).

### Fix: `latency_ms` in claude harness traces now reflects per-call duration

Previously every `llm.response` event in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl`
had `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` because the claude stream parser wrote the
cumulative wall-clock value into both fields. Per-call LLM latency was
therefore unrecoverable from captured traces.

The parser now tracks the time the most recent LLM request was sent (session
start, then reset on each `user`/tool_result event) and emits
`latency_ms = now - lastRequestSentAt` for each assistant turn. `elapsed_ms`
remains cumulative, so for any event past turn 1 the two values diverge.

Legacy traces produced before this commit have `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` and
should be ignored by latency-analysis tooling for runs predating the fix.

Regression coverage: `TestParseClaudeStreamLatencyIsPerCall` in
`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go` drives two assistant turns through
the parser with real sleeps and asserts (a) `latency_ms != elapsed_ms` for
turn 2, (b) the second-turn latency excludes the prior turn and tool window,
and (c) the sum of per-call latencies does not exceed the final event's
`elapsed_ms`.

### work: conflict-recovery for preserved iterations before discard

Bead ddx-0097af14. When a land attempt produces a merge conflict the executor
preserves the agent's commit under `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<tip>` and
the loop previously reopened the bead with status `land_conflict` — forcing a
full re-execution from scratch and discarding hours of agent work.

The loop now attempts to reuse the preserved commit before discarding it:

1. **3-way ort auto-merge (-X ours).** On `land_conflict` (and on
   `execution_failed` when the agent produced a commit before timing out), the
   loop runs `git merge --no-ff -s ort -X ours` to merge the preserved
   iteration onto the current target tip. Mechanical positional drift from
   parallel beads (the common case) resolves cleanly; the bead lands without
   operator intervention and closes normally. The merge commit preserves both
   the current-tip history and the preserved iteration as a parent, so replay
   fidelity is maintained.

2. **Focused conflict-resolve agent (optional).** If the ort auto-merge fails,
   the loop calls the optional `ConflictResolver` hook (a cheap-tier agent run
   tasked solely with resolving conflicts in the preserved files). On success
   the bead closes; on failure the hook signals whether a human is required
   (`isBlocking=true`) or the conflict may be retried.

3. **Structured park outcome.** Only after both (1) and (2) fail does the loop
   park the bead. The new status `land_conflict_unresolvable` (or
   `land_conflict_needs_human` when the focused agent returned BLOCKING)
   replaces the previous generic re-open so dashboards can distinguish
   machine-resolvable retries from conflicts that need human input. A structured
   `kind:land-conflict-unresolvable` (or `kind:land-conflict-needs-human`)
   bead event records `preserve_ref`, `base_rev`, `result_rev`, and the
   auto-merge error for operator forensics.

4. **Short 15-minute cooldown.** Unlike the 24h cap used for `push_failed`,
   `land_conflict_unresolvable` parks the bead for 15 minutes — land conflicts
   typically unblock themselves as sibling beads advance the base branch.

5. **Timeout-with-preserved-commit case.** The same recovery path fires for
   `execution_failed` when `ResultRev != BaseRev` and `PreserveRef` is set
   (i.e. the agent produced commits before the 3-hour hard timeout). Previously
   those runs were silently discarded.

**New constants:**
- `ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable` = `"land_conflict_unresolvable"`
- `ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman` = `"land_conflict_needs_human"`
- `LandConflictCooldown` = 15 min

Regression coverage:

- `TestLandConflictAutoRecover_OrtResolvesCleanly` in
  `execute_bead_land_test.go` exercises the `landConflictAutoRecover` function
  against a real git repo with a content conflict, asserting ort -X ours
  produces a clean merge commit reachable from both the current tip and the
  preserved iteration.
- `TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_AutoRecoverySucceeds_BeadCloses` (AC #6a):
  stub auto-recover returns a tip → loop closes the bead as success.
- `TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_AutoRecoverFails_EscalatesResolver` (AC #6b):
  stub auto-recover fails → loop calls `ConflictResolver` → both fail → bead
  parks with `land_conflict_unresolvable` under the 15-min cooldown.
- `TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_WithPreservedCommit_AttemptsRecovery`
  (AC #6c): `execution_failed` with `PreserveRef` and `ResultRev != BaseRev` →
  `conflictAutoRecoverFn` is called before discarding the run.
- `TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_EmptyProjectRoot_SkipsRecovery`: empty
  `ProjectRoot` bypasses recovery and preserves pre-patch `land_conflict`
  behavior for callers without a project root.

### work: recoverable push races and a 24h cap on loop-set cooldowns

Bead ddx-a458af7c. Two related fixes:

1. **Push-race auto-recovery.** When a worker's commits land locally but `git
   push` is rejected because origin advanced (the common multi-worker /
   multi-operator case), the land coordinator now attempts one
   fetch + merge + retry-push pass before giving up. Previously the bead
   was parked for a year and required manual operator intervention even
   though the work was already done locally and the merge was trivial. On a
   clean auto-merge the land returns with `PushRecovered=true` and the bead
   closes normally; the operator never sees the race.

2. **`push_conflict` is a distinct, structured outcome.** When the
   auto-recovery merge hits a real conflict (overlapping changes that the
   loop cannot resolve), Land() reports `PushConflict=true` and the loop
   maps it to a new `push_conflict` execute-bead status — distinct from the
   generic `push_failed` (and from `execution_failed`). The loop appends a
   `kind:push-conflict` bead event carrying the conflict context (detail,
   `base_rev`, `result_rev`, `session_id`) so an operator can find and
   resolve it without combing logs.

3. **Loop-set cooldowns cap at 24h.** Previously several paths
   (`push_failed`, `declined_needs_decomposition`, `review-manual-required`)
   parked beads for 365 days, which effectively meant "never retry." That
   should be a deliberate operator decision via `ddx bead update --set
   work-retry-after=...`, not an automatic loop output. A new
   constant `MaxLoopCooldown = 24h` and helper `capLoopCooldown` now clamp
   every `SetExecutionCooldown` call the loop makes. Operators extending a
   cooldown manually beyond 24h still works — the cap only governs
   loop-driven cooldowns.

   Note that this also changes the `declined_needs_decomposition` cooldown
   shipped earlier in this Unreleased section (the 365-day park described
   above); under the new cap, decomposition-declined beads re-surface within
   24h instead of being parked for a year. Operators clearing the cooldown
   via `ddx bead cooldown clear <id>` (the recommended path) is unchanged.

Regression coverage:

- `TestLand_PushAutoRecovery_RaceWithRemoteResolved` and
  `TestLand_PushAutoRecovery_UnresolvableConflictReportsPushConflict` in
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` exercise the new fetch +
  merge + retry-push recovery against real git repositories with
  side-clones advancing origin/main between local land and push.
- `TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedCooldownCappedAt24h` and
  `TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushConflictParksAndEmitsEvent` in
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_push_failed_test.go` pin the 24h cap and
  the structured `push-conflict` event contract.
- `TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead` was updated to
  assert the new 24h cap (previously asserted >180d).

### work: structured `declined_needs_decomposition` outcome

Adds a first-class outcome distinct from `no_changes` and `execution_failed`
for executors that conclude a bead is too large to deliver in one pass and
must first be split into sub-beads. Previously this case was conveyed via

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev
